overview.value: NASA Headquarters, in Washington, provides overall guidance and direction to the agency under the leadership of the Administrator. The headquarters coordinates work across ten field centers and installations nationwide. It is organized into five Mission Directorates covering aeronautics, exploration systems development, science, space operations, and space technology.

focus.value: NASA explores the unknown in air and space, innovates for the benefit of humanity, and inspires the world through discovery.

events.headline: NASA officially begins operations under the National Aeronautics and Space Act
NASA selects Boeing and SpaceX for CCtCap commercial crew transportation contracts
NASA astronauts launch on SpaceX Crew Dragon Demo-2, the first crewed flight of a commercial spacecraft
NASA and eight founding nations sign the Artemis Accords
NASA selects SpaceX to develop the Human Landing System for Artemis
NASA awards Blue Origin, Nanoracks, and Northrop Grumman contracts to develop commercial LEO destinations
